mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or remains near zero, equity discount rates stay suppressed and risk appetite improves, so S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive. The signal is strongest when inflation is not re-accelerating.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is flat or declining, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for CPIAUCSL trend.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and earnings expectations, which lowers risk appetite and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears once the unemployment rise is known.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales and housing starts together indicate broad domestic demand strength, which supports revenue growth expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint high-RSAFS/high-HOUST state does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not above baseline in the top demand-growth quartile.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while PAYEMS does not, firms are extracting more output from existing labor, which signals margin-friendly productivity gains and positive short-horizon equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while PAYEMS is flat or lower over the prior month
failure_condition: Rejected if the indicated productivity-up/labor-flat configuration is followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the interaction term is not significant.

mechanism_narrative: A positive inflation surprise in CPIAUCSL combined with unchanged FEDFUNDS implies policy is too easy relative to price pressures, which should compress real rates and support equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL accelerates for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS stays unchanged
failure_condition: Rejected if CPI acceleration without a FEDFUNDS response is followed by systematically negative 20-day SPX returns, or if returns do not exceed baseline in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 after prior expansion signals weakening final demand and lower forward earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rejected if quarterly GDPC1 contraction is not associated with below-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign is positive in most contraction episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count together with rising UNRATE identifies an employment deterioration shock that weakens consumption expectations and pushes S&P 500 forward returns negative within one month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines over 2 months and UNRATE rises over the same window
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint employment-deterioration state yields positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the interaction adds no explanatory power versus PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recovery, rising HOUST indicates future construction activity, jobs, and spending, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next five trading days when housing starts break to a new 3-month high.
failure_condition: Rejected if new-3-month-high housing starts are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the hit rate is indistinguishable from unconditional baseline.
